cve_remediation_workflow.overview: Complete workflow for CVE remediation using Concert API and GitHub integration. This workflow focuses on package manifest updates only. Unit test and code remediation will be handled separately in a different workflow.

phase.name: issue_identification
phase.title: Identify CVE Issues
phase.description: Fetch and analyze GitHub issues assigned to the DevOps engineer

steps.step: Use GitHub MCP list_issues with assignee filter
Parse issue descriptions for CVE IDs (CVE-YYYY-XXXXX format)
Extract package names and application context
Identify priority based on severity labels

phase.output: List of CVE issues with extracted metadata (CVE ID, package, application)

phase.name: concert_research
phase.title: Research CVE in Concert
phase.description: Query Concert API to understand vulnerability and get recommendations

step.action: Source .env file to load Concert credentials
List applications to find target application

filters.filter: Filter by CVE ID if available

phase.output: Concert's recommended package version and upgrade path

phase.name: branch_creation
phase.title: Create Remediation Branch
phase.description: Create a dedicated branch for the CVE fix BEFORE making any changes

critical_workflow.rule: ALWAYS create and checkout the branch BEFORE making any file changes
DO NOT modify files on main/master branch
All changes must be made on the fix branch

step.action: Use GitHub MCP create_branch tool to create the branch remotely
Checkout the newly created branch locally
step.note: This ensures all subsequent changes are made on the fix branch

step.action: Verify you are on the correct branch

alternative_approach.description: If GitHub MCP is unavailable, use local git commands

phase.name: package_update
phase.title: Update Package Manifests (ON FIX BRANCH)
phase.description: Apply Concert's recommended package version to manifest files. CRITICAL: Ensure you are on the fix branch before making changes.

pre_change_verification.check: Verify current branch is the fix branch (not main/master)
pre_change_verification.abort_if: Current branch is main or master

scope_restrictions.restriction: ONLY modify package manifest files (requirements.txt, package.json, pom.xml, etc.)
DO NOT modify application source code
DO NOT run unit tests or validation commands
If package upgrade appears to require code changes, document this in the PR but DO NOT attempt to fix it in this mode

manifest.format:
# Before {package}=={old_version} # CVE-{CVE-ID}
# After {package}=={new_version} # Fixed CVE-{CVE-ID} per Concert recommendation

steps.step: Verify you are on the fix branch (not main)
Apply Concert's recommended version using apply_diff or write_to_file

phase.name: commit_and_push
phase.title: Commit and Push Changes (ON FIX BRANCH)
phase.description: Commit package updates with detailed message and push to the fix branch

commit_message_format.example:
fix: upgrade flask to 2.3.3 for CVE-2023-12345
- Addresses CVE-2023-12345 in flask - Upgraded from 2.0.1 to 2.3.3 - Concert recommendation: Upgrade to 2.3.3 or later - Resolves #456

phase.name: pr_creation
phase.title: Create Pull Request
phase.description: Create PR with comprehensive CVE and Concert details

steps.step: Set head branch to remediation branch

completion_message_format.requirement: When completing the task, ALWAYS extract and mention the PR URL and number from the MCP tool response
completion_message_format.example: ✅ CVE remediation complete: - Created PR #39: 🔒 Security Fix: CVE-2025-27516 - Upgrade jinja2 to 3.1.6 - PR URL: https://github.com/highorbit25/concert-bob-remediate/pull/39 - Branch: fix/cve-2025-27516-jinja2 - Package: jinja2 upgraded from 3.0.3 to 3.1.6 - Resolves issue #38
completion_message_format.critical_note: The PR URL MUST be extracted from the "url" or "html_url" field in the create_pull_request MCP tool response JSON. Example: "url": "https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo/pulls/39" This allows the GitHub Actions workflow to reliably parse and extract the PR number for subsequent steps.

scope_boundaries.title: What This Mode Does and Does NOT Do

in_scope.item: Fetch GitHub issues assigned to the user
Research CVE details in Concert API
Get package upgrade recommendations from Concert
Update package manifest files (requirements.txt, package.json, pom.xml, etc.)
Create pull requests with CVE details

scope_boundaries.rationale: This mode focuses exclusively on the package upgrade decision and manifest updates. Code remediation and test fixes are separate concerns handled in different workflows. CI/CD will identify any issues that need follow-up work.

error.detection: Concert API returns 5xx errors or times out

handling.step: Proceed with manual CVE research using public databases
Note in PR that Concert was unavailable

error.detection: GitHub MCP tools fail or return errors

handling.step: Provide manual git commands as fallback
Document the steps user needs to take manually

error.detection: Package upgrade version indicates potential breaking changes

handling.step: Document the potential breaking changes in PR description
Note that CI/CD will validate compatibility
Proceed with the upgrade as recommended by Concert
Do NOT attempt to fix code issues preemptively

before_starting.item: Confirmed GitHub issue is assigned to user
Extracted CVE ID and package name

before_committing.item: Checked no application code was modified
Checked no test files were modified

before_pr_creation.item: PR notes that CI/CD will validate changes
